title: Rust announce GeoCoords::from_app_data lacks e7 range validation (Python/C reject out-of-range)
description: rust/lichen-node/src/gradient.rs:57-67 accepts any i32 (lat raw INT32_MAX -> 214.7 degrees). Python announce/coords.py:85-88 returns None out-of-range; C apps/gateway/src/network_location.c:35-38,363 returns -EINVAL with identical bounds (+-900000000 / +-1800000000). Robustness consistency, not a wire break. Also stale doc comment at gradient.rs:55 says '(Type 0x01, 8 bytes)' - the wire record is 9 bytes (type + BE int32 lat_e7 + BE int32 lon_e7). Fix: add the same bounds check + correct the comment.
